markets.question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: The recent delay of Grand Theft Auto VI to November 19, 2026 has extended the window for multiple high-profile events to occur first, shaping trader focus on outcomes like a Russia-Ukraine ceasefire or Drake’s Iceman album drop. Rockstar Games cited the need for additional polish following two prior postponements from the original 2025 target, a move that aligns with the studio’s track record of prioritizing quality over speed. This longer timeline heightens uncertainty around pre-release catalysts, including Take-Two’s upcoming earnings call for status updates and potential trailer or preorder announcements. Market-implied odds reflect aggregated sentiment that geopolitical resolutions or major music releases could land well before launch, underscoring how extended development windows amplify volatility in entertainment-adjacent prediction markets.

markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The recent mistrial in Harvey Weinstein’s New York rape retrial, declared after a deadlocked jury on May 15, 2026, has sharply elevated the market’s 77.1% implied probability on no prison time. Jurors split 9-3 for acquittal on the remaining charge involving Jessica Mann, leaving the case in limbo and reducing the odds of an additional conviction in Manhattan. While Weinstein, 74, continues serving a 16-year California sentence and faces pending New York sentencing on a separate count that could reach 25 years, the stalled retrial and his ongoing health issues have tilted trader sentiment toward limited further incarceration. Upcoming decisions on appeals or a potential fourth trial could still shift momentum, but the latest deadlock has solidified the current frontrunner outcome.

markets.question: Will the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup based off the rules of the NHL.

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NHL Stanley Cup championship.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Colorado Avalanche and Carolina Hurricanes anchor the 2026 Stanley Cup futures as the clear frontrunners, buoyed by the Avalanche’s league-best 121 regular-season points and elite core featuring Nathan MacKinnon and Cale Makar, alongside the Hurricanes’ deepest roster and perfect 8-0 playoff start through consecutive sweeps. Their Western and Eastern Conference Finals appearances keep implied probabilities tightly bunched, reflecting comparable championship experience and roster health entering the final stages. The Vegas Golden Knights’ unexpected advancement provides a credible dark-horse threat in the West, while the Montreal Canadiens and Buffalo Sabres trail as underdogs whose realistic paths hinge on extended upsets against stronger opposition. Trader consensus captures this balanced landscape, where any single injury or series swing could meaningfully shift the hierarchy.

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion

eventMetadata.context_description: The Colorado Avalanche and Carolina Hurricanes anchor the 2026 Stanley Cup futures as the clear frontrunners, buoyed by the Avalanche’s league-best 121 regular-season points and elite core featuring Nathan MacKinnon and Cale Makar, alongside the Hurricanes’ deepest roster and perfect 8-0 playoff start through consecutive sweeps. Their Western and Eastern Conference Finals appearances keep implied probabilities tightly bunched, reflecting comparable championship experience and roster health entering the final stages. The Vegas Golden Knights’ unexpected advancement provides a credible dark-horse threat in the West, while the Montreal Canadiens and Buffalo Sabres trail as underdogs whose realistic paths hinge on extended upsets against stronger opposition. Trader consensus captures this balanced landscape, where any single injury or series swing could meaningfully shift the hierarchy.

events.title: 2026 NBA Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NBA Finals.

eventMetadata.context_description: Oklahoma City Thunder's league-best 64-18 regular-season record and dominant playoff run have anchored their position as the clear frontrunner in the 2026 NBA title market, reflecting traders' view of their depth, defensive efficiency, and experience navigating the Western Conference bracket. The San Antonio Spurs trail closely behind after posting the West's second-best mark at 62-20, buoyed by Victor Wembanyama's continued impact and a strong supporting cast that has kept them competitive in early postseason games. New York Knicks have climbed on the strength of a 53-29 campaign and recent Eastern Conference momentum, while Detroit Pistons and Cleveland Cavaliers sit further back despite solid 60-22 and 52-30 finishes, limited by tougher paths through the East and less proven championship pedigrees at this stage.

markets.question: Will MegaETH perform an airdrop by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if MegaETH launches a token and performs an airdrop by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market "locked" tokens or non-swappable tokens will not suffice to resolve this market to "Yes". Airdrops of NFTs will not qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market is on-chain information and official information from MegaETH, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

markets.question: Will Spain win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The tightly bunched probabilities among the top contenders reflect the exceptional depth and recent consistency of several elite squads heading into the expanded 48-team tournament co-hosted across North America. France has edged ahead through strong recent form and a balanced roster, while Spain maintains near parity on the back of their European Championship success and attacking talent. England sits just behind, buoyed by domestic league strength and solid qualification results, with Brazil and Argentina close thanks to star experience and historical pedigree. Competitive group-stage paths and minimal rest advantages for most teams have kept the race fluid, underscoring how small differences in current form and squad health could shift the implied consensus in the weeks ahead.

markets.question: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
